reviews.review_msg: goated prof. lectures are interesting. my stage 4 insta brainrot is cured

The grading, the ta, the rng in this course is definitely bad. Its disappointing that the Prof is still decent but the TA is a meh grader! At this point even a final exam would've been a fairer reflection of the learning than the badly graded final projects. If u see the TA is Fetih please drop!!!!

The professor is decent but the TA for this course Fateh does not grade team prjects fairly so beware of that. Overall the rubric and the comments of feedback do not even align since no samples or actual guidleines are provided so it is all luck based and random. The TA for this course was not helpful at all!
Prof was always talking about his dad's business and how successful he is. TA was terrible at marking and contradicted his marking.

He's a chill prof and his lectures are pretty interesting. Only annoying thing is his slides are basically copy and pasted from the textbook and in the syllabus it says you need to buy the textbook, I wouldn't recommend it. Don't love the amount of group work for this class, especially since peer ratings can't boost your own marks, but no final.

He is a very good professor. I really like that he bridges his experience as a real business person to the class room while also sharing case studies that he has direct involvement in. I really hope he continues to teach these courses at SFU. Highly recommended.

Prof. Ramnarine was one of the best professors I've had at SFU. His thorough and direct teaching style was easy to understand. The material covered in class aligned very closely with the midterm and final exam. However, the class wasn't a breeze, you have to do a lot of reading from the textbooks to fully understand the material.

Sasha had great lectures and was very informative + well versed in this field. Examples and explanations were clear. Layout of instructions for the group projects was good, though be careful as you will be randomized in groups. Inquiries regarding assignments and exams were sometimes not answered straight-fowardly though.

Amazing and engaging lectures. Outline is clear for assignments, tests, and quizzes. Lectures are recorded which is a bonus so attendance isn't mandatory. TA does mark assignments a bit harsh but non the less, one of my favourite professors by far at SFU! Would take again. He's also super knowledgeable in business so you're in good hands.

Really top notch professor, I really enjoyed the way that he lectures and his organization was really great. Would totally recommend, I wish he taught more courses honestly. Thank you so much Sasha! Sidenote, I will say specifically for this class, try to avoid the TA Ekam Kochhar as she is an very overly tough marker.

Prof is amazing. Lectures are super interesting and I always pay attention. Provides recordings of the lectures just in case you miss a few which is extremely helpful. The TA on the other hand is a rough grader. Doesn't give good feedback to justify him grading my paper harshly.
great prof

I took this course with him in fall 2023, and in my opinion there is really nothing more I could ask from a professor. Explains things extremely clearly and concisely, makes it interesting and personal with loads of examples and anecdotes that really help you understand the concepts. Exams were fair if you paid attention and studied. Thank you!

One of my favourite teachers.
-Records lectures
-Engaging lectures
-Very knowledgeable about the course material and beyond(is a lawyer)
-Cares for the students
-Gives fair tests
-Gives practice exams 
-You don't need to read the textbook(s) and instead study slides/recordings and practice exams
-Only a midterm, take-home final, one group project

Terrific professor that gave clear feedback on top of incorporating informative real life examples into his lectures. Just wished that the curving & scaling wasn't so harsh as it adversely affected my final letter grade by the end of the course.
Great prof with lots of experience.
Sasha is solid. Lots of experience and knowledge.

Sasha is the best prof I've had. His lectures are clear, concise, and to the point, ignoring anything irrelevant to the course material, while also sprinkling in interesting anecdotes about he and his father's personal experience in the restaurant business. He is always available to talk after class, and he is personable and accommodating.

Such an amazing professor! Mr. Ramnarine truly cares about the course that he teaches and inspires you to take initiative and do well in the course. Absolutely recommend him as he is one of the best profs you could ask for in SFU.

There are textbooks although you don't need them much. 
Take notes and study lecture material because that is what you are tested on. 

Professor himself is very nice and engaging.

Lectures are recorded so you could stay home if you want to

Best prof I've had at Beedie so far. He gives excellent lectures and gives fair tests. He records all his lectures which helps out when reviewing material. It is not mandatory to attend his lectures however it pays off if you do as he will test you on material brought up in class.

Sasha is one of SFU's greatest profs. You can tell that he genuinely cares about students' learning and makes efforts to ensure we understand. His lectures are easy to follow and his use of real life examples are extremely helpful. He records his lectures, so in-person attendance is not necessary. Highly recommend taking a class with him.

Sasha is a really good professor with real-life experience to complement his course content. Exams are very fair as well as the grading. It is essential to pay attention in the lecture, as Sasha outlines questions that will be on the exam, so you can prepare better. The final is not cumulative.

Sasha is a great prof. He is very generous when it comes to grading and outlines (TAs too). He is also always available for a question or a chat after lecture, and his explanations are concise and straightforward. He is also pretty funny, and his lectures are well-structured. The group work can be a luck-of-the-draw situation though
Love his sneakers under the suit.

Each team shares the same grade in the two group assignments. The 2nd grp asmt has a peer eval. to make sure that everyone participated fairly and prof will adjust the marks of those lazy and free riders. The grading policy is decided by the prof so the reviewer here that said TAs are lazy is wrong. Read each chapter to prepare for quizzes & exams.
The best professor at SFU :)

The course content itself is ok, can be alot to learn for sure, as well as many group projects and presentations needed to be done. Sasha is a very good professor, one of the best I've had so far, he explains content very well (explains many examples) and is such an understanding person. If you can take a class with Sasha, 100% do it.

He is an illustration of what a professor should be. Very articulated and cares about teaching. He separates the class into two sections: Law (45%) Ethics (35%). Law is harder but it's not a tough course. If you can write then you are in a good position. I really enjoyed his lectures and the content is applicable to everyday life.

He was a really good prof. Listen to his lectures and you will do well. His exam questions may look easy but to do well you will need to put in a lot of detail. The questions will also be similar to what he says in the lecture, my tip would be to answer the questions in a similar way as he does in his lectures.

Good Prof, horrible class. I was stuck with the worst group members and ended up doing most of the work just to receive a C on our large assignment. The TA's are lazy and won't grade anyone based on their own contribution, its all group marks. Super upsetting and frustrating class.

Sasha is one of the top professors I've had at SFU, his expectations are perfect and understands students and tries to help them when possible. His grading criteria is also very respectable and is exactly what is expected. Put an effort and you will do well (and I'm not just saying that).

I took his online class for Labour Relations, and it was great. There was no textbook! If you do the work he gives you in the class (looking at learning objectives and completing them), then the exams will be easy. He's also a super easy marker - as long as you complete what he asks of you and are semi-competent in your answers you get full marks.

Sasha ramnarine changed my perspective on how teachers should be teaching. Most teachers just seem to teach for the money but Sasha teaches because he loves the topic and definitely has a passion for teaching others. If I do badly in this class, I know that its because I didn't study enough. favourite teacher of all time, no doubt about it.

Sasha is a good prof. However, he does not let you make your own groups, nor will he do it based on making equal academic groups. My group was full of people who basically didn't care about the course, which resulted in lower grades for me. Unfair, but whatever. Other than that, he was a good prof overall - although I thought he would be better.

Sasha was one of the best profs I have ever had at Beedie. He really cares about the students and at end of semester he encouraged us to add him on Linkedin and stay in touch. What helped me study was going through his slides and TA 's slides and talk about the concepts and application to my friends. Jordan (TA) was also super helpful (and cute).

Sasha has been my favourite prof by far during my undergrad. His lectures are engaging and gives many examples. Because the midterm and final are both 40%, his lectures are extremely important to take note of the examples he gives as theyre easy to understand and can be used in exam questions.
Great professor
Out of all the professors I've had during my four years at SFU I can safely say that Sasha is the best.

Hes such a good prof that when i did poorly on the exam, I actually blamed myself. Very compelling and in depth, makes what seems like a stale topic into a fascinating one. Don't be like me and miss the first 3 classes thinking it was just an ordinary course. It really is much more than that
much depth and great examples. Such a pure warm-hearted human being! One of my best Profs ever!

Honestly he is a great professor. He is respectful and he cares about his students. Very good at teaching, communicating with his students, answers all questions with full in depth answers making sure you understand before he moves on. This man will go above and beyond to help the students understand, pass, and relate to the concepts taught in sooo

He managed to make law interesting and clear, which I didn't think was possible. He made the lectures interesting and used relevant examples to clarify complex topics. Would definitely take another class with him!

HANDS DOWN one of the best profs I've had in my 5 years at SFU. Super passionate about the content, treats you like a human being, tests are fair, and any extra help you need is there for you the second you ask. Doesn't just read from slides, translates it into connectable content. Wish every prof at SFU was like this. A+++++!
Excellent!!

It's very hard to get a professor like him tbh. He is awesome. He is genuine and he will proove like it's not just us doing this course he is equally involved. He made law easy for me atleast. And I easily scored a A in his class. The best prof in the uni :).
Great professor and very knowledgeable in the subject matter. Fair exams that are a bit tough but fair.

It is rare you come across a professor as passionate and helpful as Sasha. He clarifies concepts that are otherwise confusing. He is also a transparent marker. As a course, BUS 483 is applicable to real life. The breakdown is on the heavier side (40% presentation; 60% final), however, Sasha is very accessible for help. 10/10 recommend!
A real one!!! Wants the best for students

Super funny guy that makes lectures interesting!! He can turn boring topics more relatable and keeps the class engaged through discussion. I recommend attending lectures as the examples mentioned in class are on the midterm. Average was quite low but if you attend classes and read the textbook you'll be way above. He's reliable and offers help.
